test_not_tautological.reason: The fail-to-pass tests check for specific patterns that don't exist in the base commit (e.g., 'config' in Category type, config command declaration, Configuration category in helpGenerator). A stub or no-op wouldn't add these patterns to the source files, so the tests would fail.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The tests cover the main requirements from instruction.md: Category type includes config, config command declared, helpGenerator has Configuration category, program routes config command, session manager has configure method, browser config has viewport 1280x720, and SKILL.md has Configuration section.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (test_config_category_in_type, test_config_command_declared, etc.), grouped with comments separating 'Gates (pass_to_pass, static)', 'Gates (pass_to_pass, repo_tests)', and 'Fail-to-pass (pr_diff)' sections, and each has a docstring explaining what it checks.

structured_data_schema.reason: The task does not produce structured data output like JSON/CSV/API/DB; it modifies TypeScript source files and markdown documentation.

no_typos.reason: File paths, command names, and variable names in instruction.md are consistent with those in the test file and solution.
